resources.string: Notifications for Fauxx background browsing diversification
Generating diverse browsing activity — %d actions today
Fauxx protects your privacy by generating synthetic browsing activity in the background. Here\'s what it does:
Performs web searches across Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, and Yahoo on diverse topics
Visits a wide variety of websites to diversify your browsing profile
Generates DNS lookups for varied domains
All activity runs in the background and uses battery and data
Optionally, tell us about yourself on the next screens. This stays on your device and helps generate noise that\'s different from your real profile. Skip if you prefer uniform noise.
Fauxx will generate diverse browsing activity that\'s maximally different from your real behavioral signal. Toggle protection on from the Dashboard to start.
Helps us avoid generating activity that matches your demographic
Optional — used only to bias noise away from your demographic
We\'ll generate more noise in OTHER categories
Type specific interests not covered above (e.g., \"woodworking\", \"cryptocurrency\")
Used to compute demographic distance only
Used to generate searches and browsing activity suggesting other regions
Fauxx will perform the following background activities:
Search diverse topics on Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, and Yahoo
Visit a variety of websites to broaden your browsing profile
Generate DNS lookups across varied domains
Use battery and mobile data while running in the background
All activity is synthetic and stays on your device. No personal data is collected or transmitted.
An open-source privacy tool that poisons data broker and ad-tech profiles by generating continuous, plausible, off-demographic synthetic activity from your device.
Data that stays on your device

All personal data remains exclusively on your device and is encrypted at rest:

• Demographic profile (age, gender, interests, profession, region)
• Ad platform profile cache (scraped interest categories)
• Synthetic persona history
• Action audit logs

This data is stored in an encrypted database (SQLCipher) with a key managed by Android Keystore. It never leaves your device in any form.

Fauxx generates synthetic browsing activity to confuse tracking profiles. This means the app will:

• Perform web searches on Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, and Yahoo
• Visit categorized URLs to accumulate diverse tracker cookies
• Load diverse pages in background web views
• Resolve domain names to generate DNS query noise
• Open app store deep links for off-profile apps

All requests use randomized User-Agent headers and are rate-limited to avoid disrupting target services. Blocked domains (private networks, harmful content) are never contacted.

• No analytics or telemetry of any kind
• No crash reports sent to external services
• No advertising identifiers or tracking pixels
• No server-side accounts or cloud storage
• No data shared with third parties

Fauxx has no backend server. The app is entirely self-contained.

If enabled, the Location Signal module generates search queries and visits websites that suggest activity in regions different from your actual location, making it harder for data brokers to pinpoint where you live.

You can delete all stored data at any time via Settings > Clear All Data. This permanently removes your demographic profile, platform caches, persona history, action logs, and resets all settings to defaults. Uninstalling the app also removes all data.
Fauxx is open-source software. Source code and license details are available in the project repository.
If you find Fauxx useful, consider buying me a coffee.
To comply with Play Store policies, this build disables ad-profile poisoning and GPS location spoofing. For the full, unrestricted version of Fauxx, install from F-Droid or GitHub.
Synthetic activity will also use this language
Why does Fauxx sometimes stop and show a “Tap to resume protection” notification?

Fauxx voluntarily pauses the engine when one of your configured constraints kicks in:

• Quiet hours: if you have an active-hours window set (default 7am–11pm), Fauxx stops at the end of the window and posts a one-tap notification scheduled for the next start time.
• WiFi only: if you have “WiFi only” enabled in Settings and lose WiFi for more than 30 minutes, Fauxx releases the foreground-service slot rather than spin idle.
• Battery threshold: if your battery is below the configured threshold and the device isn't charging, Fauxx pauses until the situation changes.

Tapping the notification opens Fauxx and restarts protection. Nothing is lost — your settings, profile, persona, and action log are persistent across pauses.

If you're seeing the engine stop outside of these configured constraints, please file an issue from Settings (“File a GitHub issue”) — that's a bug, not by design.
What does “Noise Ratio” on the Dashboard mean?

It's a throughput indicator: actions today divided by 500, capped at 100%. 500 actions in a day reads as a saturated 100%.

It does NOT measure the quality of the noise — whether the synthetic activity is hitting off-profile categories, fooling profiling systems, or covering a diverse range of topics. It's just a rate gauge.

For a view of where the noise is actually going, check the Targeting screen's category-weight chart. Red bars are categories Fauxx is suppressing (because they match your real profile), green bars are categories it's boosting, gray is neutral.
How does the “Adversarial Scraper” (Layer 2) work, and why does it sometimes say “Last scraped: Never”?

Layer 2 reads your existing ad-platform interest profiles from Google Ads Settings and Facebook Ad Preferences. It uses those to find categories the ad networks have already learned about you, then tells the engine to suppress them — so the synthetic activity steers away from your real profile.

Things to know:

• Takes up to about 60 seconds: each platform has a 30-second timeout and Google + Facebook run sequentially.
• Does NOT log you in. Fauxx reads cookies that are already on your device. You need to be signed into myadcenter.google.com and Facebook's ad preferences page in your browser first, otherwise the scraper silently returns zero categories and “Last scraped” stays as “Never.”
• Read-only. The scraper never modifies, clicks, or interacts with your ad-platform settings — it just reads what's already there.
• Auto-refreshes every 7 days on WiFi once Layer 2 is enabled. “Scrape Now” is a manual override that works on cellular too.

If you're on v0.2.6 or older and “Scrape Now” doesn't seem to do anything, that's a bug fixed in v0.2.7.
Fauxx disabled Layer 2 and the engine after repeated startup hangs. Re-enable them from Targeting and Dashboard when you\'re ready to try again.
